questions.question: In the analytics → automation loop, what does analytics tell you?

options.text: Which bottlenecks to prioritize for automation
How to reduce your automation budget

questions.explanation: Analytics identifies bottlenecks. Automation fixes them. The loop: Measure → Identify bottleneck → Automate fix → Measure improvement.

questions.question: How long should you test a hypothesis before drawing conclusions?
questions.explanation: 4-6 weeks provides enough data to see trends without waiting so long that you miss the window to course-correct. 1-2 weeks is noise; 6+ months is too slow.

questions.question: You should test multiple variables simultaneously to find improvements faster.
questions.explanation: False. Test one variable at a time. If you change messaging AND add demos simultaneously, you won't know which change drove the improvement.

questions.question: When should you add a new metric to your dashboard?

options.text: Whenever you find an interesting data source
After consistently acting on all existing metrics for 8+ weeks
Monthly, to keep your dashboard fresh

questions.explanation: Earn the right to complexity. Only add metrics when you've acted on everything you already track for 8+ weeks. Each new metric must change a decision.
